2014-06-01.title: Lesson for P.O.W.’s Father: Men Sometimes Do Come Back
2014-06-01.abstract: Robert Bergdahl fought in Washington for his son’s release from the Taliban for five years. On Saturday, President Obama said Bowe Bergdahl was coming home.

2014-06-01.abstract: For Secretary of Defense Chuck Hagel, the release of the American prisoner of war is the high point of his tenure in the cabinet so far.

2014-06-02.abstract: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel called on the international community “not to run to recognize” the new Palestinian government scheduled to be sworn in Monday, saying it rests on a terrorist organization.

2014-06-06.abstract: That massacre in the village of Attagara was the bloodiest in a series of attacks by Boko Haram this week in Nigeria’s far north, according to officials.

2014-06-06.abstract: Alarmed over the rising human death toll from the Middle East Respiratory Syndrome Coronavirus, authorities in Saudi Arabia, said they would start testing the kingdom’s entire population of camels.

2014-06-07.abstract: Jane D. Hartley, chief executive officer of an economic consulting firm, will serve in France, and Kevin F. O’Malley, a lawyer, will fill a long-vacant post in Ireland.

2014-06-24.abstract: Iraqi police were said to have killed scores of Sunni insurgents, while militants turned over the bodies of Shiite civilians they had killed, only to bomb the cemetery during their funerals, according to one account.

2014-06-25.abstract: How can the Islamic State in Iraq and Syria tell whether a person is a Sunni or a Shiite? From accounts, it seems they often ask a list of questions.

2014-06-25.abstract: The Obama administration has drawn up plans to escalate sanctions against Russia by targeting its financial, energy and defense industries.

2014-06-26.title: Tense Vote Spares Polish Government After Recordings Scandal
2014-06-26.abstract: Prime Minister Donald Tusk and his administration were retained Wednesday, despite controversy over secret tapes of officials’ private conversations that surfaced last week.

2014-06-26.title: Russia and Others Seek Extended Ukraine Truce, but Separatists Balk
2014-06-26.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin said that Russia had made a commitment to ease the conflict by revoking an order that provided for the use of the Russian Army in Ukraine.

2014-06-26.title: U.S. Chided for Delays Over Treaty on Weapons
2014-06-26.abstract: Though the United States is the biggest contributor in helping to rid land mines from old conflict zones, the International Campaign to Ban Landmines said it still has not signed an international treaty.

2014-06-26.title: Beating of Roma Boy Exposes Tensions in France’s Underclass
2014-06-26.abstract: The near fatal beating of a Roma youth suspected of burglary by a gang on the outskirts of Paris has highlighted the tensions in France’s economic underclass.

2014-06-26.title: Questions Raised Over Trial for Ahmed Abu Khattala in Benghazi Case
2014-06-26.abstract: The Washington courthouse is not connected to a jail, so the government will have to move Ahmed Abu Khattala with armed guards in armored vehicles.

2014-06-27.title: In Gaza, Work but No Pay
2014-06-27.abstract: Being a government employee in Gaza does not guarantee a paycheck, as workers hired by rival political factions fight for scarce funds.

2014-06-27.title: Israel Names Prime Suspects in Abductions
2014-06-27.abstract: Describing Marwan Qawasmeh and Amer Abu Aisha as “Hamas activists,” Israel said it had been hunting for the two since the kidnapping of three teenagers came to light.

2014-06-27.title: For British Spy in Iraq, Affection Is Strong but Legacy Is Unfulfilled
2014-06-27.abstract: Gertrude Bell, who worked to stabilize Iraq after World War I, won hearts and minds but was unable to meld a mix of religious sects into a stable nation.

2014-06-27.title: Militarism and Humiliation Cast Shadow on Germany
2014-06-27.abstract: A century after World War I began, German leadership in Europe is both desired and resented, an ambivalence keenly felt by both the Germans and their wary neighbors.

2014-06-27.title: The War to End All Wars? Hardly. But It Did Change Them Forever.
2014-06-27.abstract: World War I destroyed kings, kaisers, czars and sultans; it demolished empires; it introduced chemical weapons; it brought millions of women into the work force.

2014-06-28.title: Tensions Continue to Simmer as Attacks in Gaza Escalate
2014-06-28.abstract: Two Palestinian men suspected of being militants were killed in an Israeli missile strike in Gaza on Friday, according to the military and witnesses.

2014-06-28.title: Hong Kong’s Democracy Supporters Chafe at Inequality and Beijing’s Sway
2014-06-28.abstract: An informal vote on how to select Hong Kong’s leader reflects resentment that the Beijing-oriented political-business machine is rigged against the people.

2014-06-28.title: Taliban Mount Major Assault in Afghanistan
2014-06-28.abstract: The group has attacked police outposts and government facilities in several districts of northern Helmand Province, sending the police and military officials scrambling to shore up defenses.

2014-06-28.title: Syria: Dispute Unsettles Rebel Coalition
2014-06-28.abstract: The interim government said that it would fire the head of the Supreme Military Council of the Free Syrian Army and refer its members to a commission investigating corruption.

2014-06-28.title: Leslie Manigat, Overthrown in a Coup in Haiti, Dies at 83
2014-06-28.abstract: Mr. Manigat was overthrown in a bloodless military coup after serving less than six months in office.

2014-06-29.title: North Korea Plans to Indict Two Americans; Defies U.N. by Firing Ballistic Missiles
2014-06-29.abstract: The official news agency said that the Americans would be tried on charges of committing “hostile acts,” a report that came a day after the country launched two missiles off its east coast.

2014-06-29.title: 1914: Assassination of the Archduke Francis Ferdinand was a conspiracy
2014-06-29.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archives: In 1914, the assassination of Archduke Francis Ferdinand was the outcome of a conspiracy; Dublin elects its first woman Lord Mayor in 1939.

2014-06-29.title: Hong Kong Poll Turnout Buoys Democracy Activists
2014-06-29.abstract: An unofficial referendum on democratic changes in Hong Kong attracted votes equal to more than a fifth of the city’s electorate, organizers said.

2014-06-29.title: Russian Jets and Experts Sent to Iraq to Aid Army
2014-06-29.abstract: The experts’ arrival was seen as an implicit rebuke to the United States, where concerns in Congress about the political viability of Iraq’s government have stalled aircraft sales to Iraq.

2014-06-29.title: Japanese Protester Sets Himself on Fire at Train Station in Tokyo
2014-06-29.abstract: The man set himself aflame at Tokyo’s busiest train station in what appeared to be a protest against efforts to weaken Japan’s antiwar Constitution.

2014-06-30.title: Awakening the ‘Dutch Gene’ of Water Survival
2014-06-30.abstract: The dikes of the country’s water management system work so well that experts say they worry that citizens will begin to take staying dry for granted.

2014-06-30.title: Flawed Saudi Response Is Cited in Outbreak of the Middle East Virus MERS
2014-06-30.abstract: Cases of Middle East Respiratory Syndrome have more than tripled since the end of 2013, leading health experts to cite grave flaws in the way Saudi health officials have handled the crisis.

2014-06-30.title: One Son Missing, Another Dead, and 2 Mothers Across a Divide
2014-06-30.abstract: The mothers, one Israeli and the other Palestinian, have taken on conspicuous roles in their communities because of their losses.

2014-06-30.title: After West Bank Kidnapping, 2 Mothers Embody a Divide
2014-06-30.abstract: Israel’s security crackdown has raised questions about the asymmetry of the Israeli-Palestinian conflict and the value of lives on both sides.

2014-06-30.title: Netanyahu Says Security Control Is Vital in Any Accord
2014-06-30.abstract: Pointing to the rise of Islamic extremism, the Israeli prime minister appeared to toughen his requirements, further dimming the prospects for renewed talks.
